recommendations.title: Selma
recommendations.overview: "Selma," as in Alabama, the place where segregation in the South was at its worst, leading to a march that ended in violence, forcing a famous statement by President Lyndon B. Johnson that ultimately led to the signing of the Voting Rights Act.

recommendations.title: The Lion in Winter
recommendations.overview: Henry II and his estranged queen, Eleanor of Aquitaine, battle over the choice of an heir.

recommendations.title: Milk
recommendations.overview: The true story of Harvey Milk, the first openly gay man ever elected to public office. In San Francisco in the late 1970s, Harvey Milk becomes an activist for gay rights and inspires others to join him in his fight for equal rights that should be ava

recommendations.title: Somewhere in Time
recommendations.overview: Young writer Richard Collier is met on the opening night of his first play by an old lady who begs him to "Come back to me". Mystified, he tries to find out about her, and learns that she is a famous stage actress from the early twentieth century. Be

recommendations.title: Hammamet
recommendations.overview: The last years of Bettino Craxi, one of the most important and controversial italian leader of the 1980's.
The epic tale of a class struggle in twentieth century Italy, as seen through the eyes of two childhood friends on opposing sides.

recommendations.title: Age of Uprising: The Legend of Michael Kohlhaas
recommendations.overview: In the 16th century in the Cévennes, a horse dealer by the name of Michael Kohlhaas leads a happy and prosperous family life. When a lord treats him unjustly, this pious, upstanding man raises an army and puts the country to fire and sword in order t

recommendations.title: The Duchess
recommendations.overview: A chronicle of the life of 18th century aristocrat Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire, who was reviled for her extravagant political and personal life.

recommendations.title: Malcolm X
recommendations.overview: A tribute to the controversial black activist and leader of the struggle for black liberation. He hit bottom during his imprisonment in the '50s, he became a Black Muslim and then a leader in the Nation of Islam. His assassination in 1965 left a lega

recommendations.title: Rustin
recommendations.overview: Activist Bayard Rustin faces racism and homophobia as he helps change the course of Civil Rights history by orchestrating the 1963 March on Washington.
